column1:
Priority 1 = Required for release
Priority 2 = Strongly preferred for release
Priority 3 = Deferred roll-out acceptable
Feature
column3: Notes

column1: AI Agent: instructions
column3:
See notes; discuss gaps, limitations.
Especially note #4, #5, #6

column1: AI Agent: Priority Sources to be indexed
column3:
Current standards
Deprecated standards (never to be indexed for external API use)

column1: AI Agent: Future Sources to be indexed
column3:
ies.org resources (calendar, eLearning, Leukos, etc)
IES section websites & events
Wicket: sections, technical committee rosters

column1: AI search results: 'sections'
column3: GOAL: AI agent helps user find content, but primarily displays unaltered screenshots and annotated excerpts directly from IES standards.  It should be visually clear which content on the page is directly transcribed, and what is generated by AI natural language.  For this reason, all search result examples are broken into sections; each drives user back to the relevant page of a standard in Vitrium.

column1: AI search results: natural language responses
column3: In addition to transcribed/annotated excerpts, a natural language response should be provided.  It should not appear within AI agent window at top of screen if possible, but rather below excerpts.  Require users to acknowledge a disclaimer to view. Watermark content if possible

column1: AI search results page: discourage copy/paste/print
Indexing: should allow links directly to the relevant page in Vitrium document
column3: Train on a separate database of PDFs on IES server

column1: AI Search Results: Illuminance Tables must be presented accurately and fully
column3: Discuss preferred table format for accurate machine reading.  Must be capable of extracting full header (typically 3-4 rows), relevant application rows with all columns, plus all referenced footnotes - for each search result.  Hyperlinks to table in Vitrium document.

column1: AI Search Results: highlight most relevant text in excerpts from standards
column3: AI to auto-highlight most relevant portions of transcribed content

column1: AI Search results: ability to present an image, table or graphic as a search result where relevant
column3: Example: a search for "what is the formular for ___" may return an image or screenshot of a formula from the standard

column1: AI search integrated into Vitrium
column3:
We have innate control over a bar near top of page (html etc).  We aren't 'deleting' the built-in Vitrium search so we need a graphical prominence that draws users to the AI tool.
Open search in popup??

column1: Tool to convert user's xfdf annotation file to Excel
column3: Permits limited 'download' permission for user's content and truncated quoted text

column1: Links to committee members Contributor Pages or LinkedIn
column3: If user is logged-in as IES member, they can click author names and find more information about them in a directory or their personal LinkedIn

column1: AI Search: Submit request for lighting recommendations for multiple applications in a single query
column3: See search example, page 3

column1: AI Search: document version comparison
column3: See notes on page 5.  Allow users to discover what has changed in an update to a standard.  Data regarding deprecated standards will likely will not be made available to external API calls.

column1: Integrate AI search into ies.org
column3:
Library subscribers would see full AI search results plus website search
Non-subscribers would only see recommended purchases plus website search

column1: Auto-populate webstore listings from Vitrium document metadata
Auto-populate list of committee members and their roles for webstore listings
column3: lookup 'Author'=committee in doc metadata; reference to Wicket committee roster

column1: Preview document' thumbnail/link auto-populated in webstore
column3: Staff to create a limited 'Preview' pdf for each standard; public viewing permissions

column1: Enable multi-user purchasing & licensing on webstore
Develop a way for staff to bulk-create IES accounts in order to bulk-assign Vitrium permissions
column3: Not necessary for standard purchases, but would be necessary for us to allow a full class of students or an audience to acquire viewing permissions for a 1-time event, etc.  Staff would need to enter their email addresses into Vitrium.

column1: Sustaining Oranization checkout: assign users and allow additional Lighting Library user purchase at checkout
column3: Note: each Sustaining Organization level may have a different % discount for purchasing additional Lighting Library users beyond their benefit qty
